metadata.purpose: Core-slim line-review ledger: every retained file, function, and public knob must be classified as keep, merge, delete, or extract-pr before the refactor PR is review-ready.
metadata.next_step: Execute plan_final.md file-level queues in order, starting with QI/device-QI artifacts and experimental solver route extraction, then RHSMode-1 orchestration collapse, operator cleanup, transport collapse, validation/evidence shrink, examples/tests curation, and root/API cleanup.
metadata.latest_tranche_evidence: Removed direct-tail support-mode preflight and stable-output plumbing; deleted 13 tracked generated performance-output JSON files; consolidated seven release/data/audit/hygiene scripts into validation CLIs; extracted unsupported single-case sharded and multi-GPU performance campaign examples/tests; extracted high-nu and W7-X publication-audit generators while retaining compact checked artifacts; extracted optional Lineax and Equinox/JAXopt adoption benchmark drivers; moved Krylov implementation from the package root to sfincs_jax.solvers.krylov while keeping sfincs_jax.solver as a compatibility alias; rewrote plan_final.md into a stricter finite work order and added rule-expanded whole-repo inventory coverage; deleted the duplicate root utils/ tree and promoted the enhanced noninteractive upstream utility behavior into examples/sfincs_examples/utils, leaving one canonical vendored utility location; promoted the remaining Python release/profiling scripts into sfincs_jax.validation modules so scripts/ is no longer a Python implementation area; merged validation figure-panel helpers into validation artifacts so evidence schemas and docs-panel gates have one owner; merged reduced and scaled suite runners into sfincs_jax.validation.suite with explicit reduced/scaled subcommands and legacy script-name dispatch for archived tests; consolidated production-input generation, README audit regeneration, and write-output tracing into sfincs_jax.validation.release subcommands so validation reaches five implementation modules plus __init__; removed unsupported BLR/HSS and nested-dissection frontal stable solver routes with their aliases, env knobs, metadata, tests, and docs; made plan_final.md line/file review criteria explicit for public symbols, env vars, CLI flags, namelist aliases, output keys, diagnostics fields, QI/device-QI artifacts, example folders, and test files. Focused local checks across those tranches covered source-tree/domain contracts, release/data/audit tests, scaled-suite source-tree checks, publication-audit extraction checks, package CLI checks, upstream utility smoke tests, Ruff, compileall, JSON validation, diff hygiene, and size guard.; extracted stable QI/device-QI generated artifacts, promotion tests, default validation references, and README/docs artifact claims from the stable core; compacted the research-lane manifest to existing small evidence and branch-scoped QI/device-QI as deferred research; removed four untested performance profiling/campaign scripts from stable examples and rewrote docs to point to retained output-format, structured-solve, transport-worker, and checked compile/runtime evidence instead; removed optional Optax calibration/geometry-fitting/publication-figure optimization scripts from stable examples and kept the documented optimization lane on tested QA objective, bootstrap-current comparison, and scan-promotion workflows; removed the W7-X high-nu candidate preconditioner harness and its direct test from stable examples while retaining the checked performance figure and full scan command.

extraction_groups.purpose: Preserve QI and true device-QI solver research outside the stable core.
extraction_groups.core_removal_gate: No retained core import may reference preconditioner_qi, profile_sparse_qi, or validation.qi_device.

extraction_groups.purpose: Preserve native sparse-direct, symbolic factor, true-operator rescue, and active-factor research outside the stable core.
extraction_groups.core_removal_gate: Core sparse paths must still pass strict residual gates without symbolic/native rescue imports.

extraction_groups.purpose: Preserve sharded, multi-worker, multi-GPU, and long profiling campaigns outside the stable core.
extraction_groups.core_removal_gate: Serial/default CPU and GPU solves must remain available; scaling claims move out unless fresh evidence is retained.

extraction_groups.purpose: Preserve historical benchmark campaigns, publication figures, Zenodo audits, and long-run validation scripts outside the stable core.
extraction_groups.core_removal_gate: README/docs can keep small checked figures and summaries, but long-run campaign drivers leave the core tree.

rules.name: repo_metadata_and_top_level_docs
rules.next_action: Scrub public prose after source and evidence decisions settle.

rules.name: github_ci_workflows
rules.line_target: keep only package, docs, coverage, and publish workflows
rules.next_action: Keep CI under 10 minutes while preserving coverage and docs gates.

rules.name: package_source
rules.line_target: <=50 Python files and <=50k package lines unless ledger exception
rules.next_action: Continue solver research after BLR/HSS and nested-dissection stable-route removal: remove or extract multifrontal/true-operator rescue/manual native-factor routes that are not auto-admitted, then collapse RHSMode-1 policy tables.

rules.name: compact_test_fixtures
rules.line_target: compact JSON only; large data belongs in release assets
rules.next_action: Retain fixtures only when a physics/API/docs claim test consumes them.

rules.name: reference_fixture_data
rules.line_target: deduplicate into compact fixtures or release-hosted assets
rules.next_action: Delete unused reference blobs and keep only data needed for stable claims.

rules.name: generated_suite_reports
rules.line_target: move long-run reports to release assets or research PRs
rules.next_action: Keep only compact claim fixtures required by current docs/readme.

rules.name: reduced_test_inputs
rules.line_target: minimal representative inputs; no historical duplicates
rules.next_action: Parametrize tests over fewer canonical input fixtures.

rules.name: python_tests
rules.proof_owner: pytest coverage and domain regression suites
rules.next_action: Delete tests that only pin extracted research paths; merge the retained tests into parametrized physics, numerics, API, autodiff, parity, and docs-claim suites.

rules.name: docs_static_evidence
rules.proof_owner: docs-claim tests and validation figure schema tests
rules.line_target: compressed checked figures and compact JSON only
rules.next_action: Delete stale campaign artifacts and regenerate retained figures from stable workflows.

rules.name: upstream_docs_sources
rules.line_target: retain only cited source material needed for stable docs
rules.next_action: Move bulky or uncited upstream material to external references.

rules.name: public_docs
rules.line_target: standalone user docs with no branch-history prose
rules.next_action: Scrub unsupported research claims after code extraction.

rules.name: original_sfincs_examples
rules.line_target: retain original v3 references needed for parity examples
rules.next_action: Keep as reference inputs; do not add generated outputs.

rules.name: publication_and_performance_examples
rules.line_target: move long generators/campaigns out of stable examples
rules.next_action: Move long publication/performance generators to preservation branches; keep only compact checked figures and their source data when public docs still cite them.

rules.name: optimization_examples
rules.line_target: at most one curated optimization workflow in stable examples
rules.next_action: Extract unpromoted campaigns; keep one pedagogical optimization example.

rules.name: curated_examples
rules.proof_owner: examples tree contract and smoke-free API tests
rules.line_target: <=10 curated workflows outside original v3 references
rules.next_action: Reduce to original v3 references plus no more than 10 teaching workflows: CLI solve/plot, Python solve, output formats, transport, bootstrap/Redl, ambipolar root, autodiff, geometry loading, optimization, and validation comparison.

rules.name: temporary_scripts
rules.proof_owner: script import and release/data CLI tests
rules.line_target: empty or documented release/data tooling only
rules.next_action: Convert useful scripts into package CLIs/tests/examples; delete the rest.

rules.name: legacy_utils
rules.next_action: Delete obsolete upstream utilities or move any documented user workflow into examples.

entries.retention_reason: RHSMode-1 automatic solver policy is core, but experimental policy branches and duplicated route predicates must be removed or extracted.
entries.next_step: Separate parity-clean default route predicates from research/probe routes, then delete unreferenced helpers.

section_cards.reason: public/default policy readers and stable automatic selection helpers
duplicate sparse/direct/rescue policy predicates must collapse into one RHSMode-1 policy table
stage-2, scipy rescue, sparse rescue, and hidden-env route probes are not stable user API
backend-specific wrappers should reduce to one automatic default policy surface

entries.retention_reason: The promoted x-block/tz sparse route is part of the current automatic RHSMode-1 path, but lower-confidence variants should move to research.
entries.next_step: Keep the strict residual-clean route and extract fallback experiments to research/native-sparse-direct.

section_cards.reason: rescue/seed/global/high-x correction experiments are not a single stable default route
x-block matvec, initial guess, assembled preflight, and stable sparse-PC setup
metadata builders and route-state dictionaries duplicate output and policy owners
Krylov execution and final residual gate remain until RHSMode-1 pipeline collapse

entries.retention_reason: RHSMode-1 full-system operator assembly is core physics/numerics and must remain readable.
entries.next_step: Remove unused assembly variants, collapse duplicated layout glue, and keep equation blocks close to their tests.

section_cards.reason: structured RHSMode-1 CSR assembly and active projected full-system preconditioner
coupled kinetic, symbolic frontal/superblock/block-schur, and coupled-schur experiments
native x-ell and low-l/ell-band/window coarse variants must reduce to admitted defaults
tail assembly and source/constraint/Phi1 helpers used by structured full CSR

entries.retention_reason: True device-QI remains a research lane and should not be shipped in the slim core.
entries.next_step: Preserve on research branch, remove from core imports, and keep only a deferred-lane documentation pointer.

entries.retention_reason: Sparse CSR primitives needed by default solvers are core; native direct-factor experiments should be extracted.
entries.next_step: Keep CSR construction/solve primitives with strict tests and move symbolic/native factor experiments out.

section_cards.reason: CSR matvec, triangular solves, storage estimates, and factor admission contracts
symbolic block/coarse/schur/frontal/superblock factors are native sparse-direct research
operator construction, pattern coloring, host direct solve, and refinement should keep only stable default paths

entries.retention_reason: Sparse RHSMode-1 solve orchestration is core only for parity-clean automatic routes.
entries.next_step: Continue collapsing generic branch setup and direct-tail policy wiring after disconnected true-operator rescue stages and direct-tail support-mode preflight were removed.
entries.justification: Stable sparse-PC orchestration keeps factor-preflight flow; disconnected direct-tail residual/true-active/coupled-coarse rescue policy expansion and support-mode preflight were removed.

entries.retention_reason: QI solve variants are not part of the stable core until production residual/runtime gates pass.
entries.next_step: Extract with QI tests, examples, and docs to a research PR.

entries.retention_reason: QI correction experiments are explicitly deferred research.
entries.next_step: Move as a family with QI device code and remove core route references.

entries.retention_reason: RHSMode-1 top-level solve orchestration is core, but should read as setup -> operator -> solve -> diagnostics.
entries.next_step: Remove extracted route hooks and collapse compatibility wrappers.

entries.retention_reason: RHSMode-2/3 transport matrix linear systems are core for transport coefficients.
entries.next_step: Retain default system build/solve; move experimental factor/preconditioner branches out.

section_cards.reason: transport linear solve, dense batch solve, and active dense setup
active block-Schur factor and residual coarse correction are admitted transport defaults
direct reduced-Pmat symbolic/native sparse controls and hidden-env variants need research extraction or collapse

entries.retention_reason: QI basis/coarse-space experiments are not core.
entries.next_step: Extract with QI preconditioner family.

entries.retention_reason: Native sparse-direct replacement remains a research lane until it is smaller, faster, and production-clean.
entries.next_step: Extract with symbolic/native sparse factor code.
entries.justification: Stable direct-tail direct/structured preconditioner helpers remain; residual/true-active/coupled-coarse rescue policies, support-mode preflight dataclasses, and resolvers were deleted from stable core.

entries.retention_reason: Parallel scaling campaigns are not required for the slim core CLI/API and remain research/performance infrastructure.
entries.next_step: Extract long-run worker/sharded paths; keep only any proven simple CPU/GPU backend defaults.

entries.retention_reason: Dense RHSMode-1 path is a validated small/medium default and fallback.
entries.next_step: Keep simple dense solve and remove research admission/probe branches.

entries.retention_reason: Public solver facade and generic Krylov helpers are core but should not contain policy experiments.
entries.next_step: Keep sfincs_jax.solver as a small compatibility alias; trim unused Krylov branches inside sfincs_jax.solvers.krylov.

entries.retention_reason: RHSMode-1 output fields and diagnostics are core output-schema functionality.
entries.next_step: Remove solver-policy hooks that disappear with extracted research routes.

entries.retention_reason: Validation suite commands are useful release tooling, but long reduced/scaled campaign orchestration should not own core solve logic.
entries.next_step: Keep compact parser/report helpers and public reduced/scaled command shims; extract long campaign generation and duplicated runner plumbing.

section_cards.reason: case result schema, parsers, resolution helpers, and compact log/metric readers
single-case runner and Fortran/JAX comparison helpers should be shared by reduced and scaled commands
reduced/scaled campaign drivers are release tooling, not importable solver architecture
scaled-suite command and dispatcher should reduce to small public CLI wrappers

entries.retention_reason: Small validation fixture checks and docs-panel evidence builders are core; research-lane, benchmark, and publication campaign validators should move out.
entries.next_step: Keep schema checks and compact figure-panel evidence used by retained docs/tests; extract publication/benchmark campaign validators and reduce this owner below 900 lines.

entries.retention_reason: True-operator rescue is a research fallback, not a default stable solver path.
entries.next_step: Extract with native sparse-direct work and remove default-policy hooks.

entries.retention_reason: Examples are user education, but the release core should contain only Fortran-v3 references and at most 10 curated workflows.
entries.next_step: Move performance, publication, QI, and one-off audits to research PRs or release assets.

entries.retention_reason: Tests must prove the slim core, not preserve every historical experiment.
entries.next_step: Reorganize into unit, physics, regression, cli_io, fixtures, and integration_optional after extraction.

entries.retention_reason: Long benchmark campaigns do not belong in the installable/review core.
entries.next_step: Keep only small summary fixtures and one docs-consistency test in core.

entries.retention_reason: Python implementation scripts are not part of the stable architecture; scripts may contain only documented shell or release-maintenance tooling.
entries.next_step: Keep the Fortran MPI shell wrapper only while documented, otherwise move shell release tooling into docs or delete scripts/ entirely.

entries.retention_reason: Stable performance examples keep output-format, JIT, transport-worker, structured-solve, and bounded parity/runtime evidence; unsupported sharded, multi-GPU, and optional ecosystem adoption drivers have been extracted.
entries.next_step: Continue shrinking performance examples toward one output-format workflow plus one documented transport-worker benchmark; remaining long profiling drivers should become package validation tooling or research-lane material.

entries.retention_reason: Publication figure regeneration is valuable but not required in the slim user-facing core.
entries.next_step: Move figure generation campaigns to publication-audits PR and keep checked final figures only if small.

entries.retention_reason: QI device artifact validation supports a deferred research lane.
entries.next_step: Extract with QI device branch and remove from core validation surface.
